mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is continuous and does not require a discrete threshold to activate.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or periods of falling FEDFUNDS show no statistically significant excess return versus the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above a critical threshold indicating a deteriorating labor market, investors reprice near-term corporate earnings downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week as the market digests the signal quickly.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 6.0% on the most recent monthly reading
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, the subsequent 5-day forward SPX return is positive, or the average forward return in triggered instances is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint contraction in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates a synchronized pullback in consumer discretionary and durable-goods spending that historically precedes broader economic slowdowns, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down across cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST post month-over-month declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Joint two-month declines in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by positive 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental rank IC versus either series used alone.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, decelerating INDPRO growth signals an approaching industrial slowdown that the equity market has historically under-priced, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as the deceleration becomes broadly recognized. In early-cycle or recovery regimes the same deceleration carries no reliable directional signal because base effects dominate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: During periods independently classified as late-cycle expansion, INDPRO deceleration is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances, or the sign of the effect reverses across two consecutive late-cycle episodes.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between rising CPIAUCSL inflation and a low or falling FEDFUNDS rate implies a deeply negative real policy rate, which encourages risk-asset allocation and equity multiple expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. Neither series alone captures this effect because it depends on the real-rate gap.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change minus FEDFUNDS implies a real rate below -1.0 percentage points
failure_condition: When the computed real rate proxy is below -1.0pp, forward 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction model's rank IC does not exceed the best single-series model's rank IC by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in PAYEMS signals healthy labor demand and consumer income generation, supporting near-term consumption and corporate revenue expectations, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the following week. This is a continuously active trend effect requiring no threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS change and forward 5-day SPX return remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of PAYEMS growth show no significant excess return versus periods of PAYEMS contraction.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below a critical threshold consistent with sub-trend expansion, forward-looking earnings estimates are revised down over the following quarter, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market prices in slower aggregate demand.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: Instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5% are followed by positive 60-day forward SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect is not distinguishable from the unconditional base rate of positive returns (chi-square p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where INDPRO is contracting while PAYEMS growth remains positive, the divergence signals an early-stage industrial slowdown not yet reflected in labor markets; this combination historically precedes negative equity returns over the next month as the labor market subsequently catches down to weaker production. The signal requires the joint condition and is absent in expansions where both series move together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline while PAYEMS month-over-month change remains positive
failure_condition: When the INDPRO-down/PAYEMS-up divergence occurs, forward 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the joint-condition rank IC does not exceed the rank IC of INDPRO alone by at least 0.02.
